mnemonics: DF stands for Degrees of Freedom - think 'Data Flexibility' - how many values are free to vary given statistical constraints.
keyPoints: Degrees of freedom represent the number of independent pieces of information available to estimate a parameter.
In many models, df equals the number of observations minus the number of parameters being estimated.
Degrees of freedom affect the shape of distributions like the t-distribution and chi-square distribution.
commonMistakes: Confusing degrees of freedom with sample size - they are related but not the same.
Forgetting to adjust degrees of freedom when multiple parameters are estimated from the same data.
Using the wrong degrees of freedom value for hypothesis tests and confidence intervals.
